heading: Reserves: kinds of duty
label: 12314 Reserves: kinds of duty
text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a member of a reserve component who is on active duty other than for training may, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary concerned, be detailed or assigned to any duty authorized by law for members of the regular component of the armed force concerned.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)68250:965., .July 9, 1952, ch. 608, § 23766 Stat. 492The words “armed force concerned” are substituted for the words “Armed Forces of the United States”. The words “now or hereafter” and “officers and enlisted” are omitted as surplusage. The words “other than for training” are inserted, since the words “active duty” were defined in the source statute cited above to exclude active duty for training.
Amendments1994— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 103–337section 682 of this title
